source.title: Multiple analyst estimates on PDD GMV and take rate (Bernstein, Morningstar, Momentum Works)

extractions.claim: PDD's domestic (Pinduoduo) take rate estimated at ~4.2% in FY2023, up from 3.6% in FY2021 — still below Alibaba's ~5%
extractions.evidence: Bernstein estimates PDD's take rate more than tripled between FY17 to FY21 to 3.6%, reaching 4.2% in FY23. One analyst thesis suggests PDD's main app could increase its take rate to 5% (matching Alibaba) with ~RMB 4.7T GMV. This would imply domestic revenue of ~RMB 220B.

verification.verifier_notes: Bernstein estimate cited in multiple analyst reports. Take rate = total revenue / GMV. PDD stopped disclosing GMV after FY2021 (last disclosed: RMB 2.44T in FY2021), so all subsequent GMV and take rate figures are analyst estimates.

extractions.claim: Temu's effective take rate is structurally different from Pinduoduo: 35-40% for fully managed, low double-digits for semi-managed
extractions.evidence: Bernstein estimates Temu take rate around 35% of GMV under fully managed model (Temu buys at supply price, sets retail price, keeps the spread), and low double digits under semi-managed (merchant ships, Temu takes commission). Temu GMV estimates: ~$14-15B in 2023, $30-60B in 2024 (wide range), with semi-managed now ~34% of global transaction volume.

verification.verifier_notes: Temu does not disclose GMV or take rate. These are Bernstein analyst estimates. The wide range in GMV estimates ($30B-$60B for 2024) reflects genuine uncertainty.

extractions.claim: PDD stopped disclosing GMV after FY2021 — all subsequent GMV figures are analyst estimates with wide variance
extractions.evidence: Last official GMV disclosure: Pinduoduo FY2021 GMV of RMB 2.44 trillion. PDD has not disclosed GMV since. Analyst estimates for FY2024 combined GMV range from $400B to $600B+ depending on methodology.

verification.verifier_notes: Confirmed via PDD's 20-F filings. GMV disclosure stopped after FY2021.
